name: AdFeed
description: In-feed advertisement card. Rides the same scrolling column as Feed but trades the channel/author header for a brand row (avatar + brand name + tag-line subtitle + a trailing close affordance), drops the engagement footer, and commits a single full-width primary call-to-action under a hero media block. Slots beyond the brand row and CTA are optional — a minimal placement renders brand row + CTA over the hero image, while a fully-authored placement adds a title and body excerpt above the media.

brand.description: { name, subtitle?, avatar? } — leading brand row. `avatar` is forwarded to Thumbnail verbatim at size 32; omit `avatar.src` to render the surface-toned fallback. `subtitle` is optional (e.g. 'Sponsored', 'Blind Company Reviews').

onDismiss.description: Fires when the trailing close (X) is tapped. Omit to render the card without a dismiss affordance.

dismissLabel.description: Accessible label for the trailing close button. Defaults to 'Dismiss ad'.

title.description: Ad headline. heading.md (20 / Semibold) / onSurface. Single line; truncates with ellipsis.

body.description: Ad body copy. body.sm (14 / Regular) / onSurfaceVariant. Two-line clamp with trailing ellipsis.

media.description: { src, alt, background? } — hero media block. `background` is an optional CSS color used as the card fill behind the image (matches the brand-tinted block in the mock when no `src` is supplied or when the image is a cut-out).

cta.description: { label, onClick?, appearance?, size? } — the single full-width Standard Button at the foot. `appearance` defaults to `primary` and `size` to `medium`. Omit on placements that surface inside a destination the user already chose (e.g. the My Page surface), where the entire card is the affordance and a redundant CTA would compete with the surface's own navigation.

brand.description: Top row: 32-rung Thumbnail + a two-line text block (brand name on top, optional subtitle underneath). The row is non-interactive at the container level — the brand and subtitle are display text, not links — so the only commit in this region is the trailing close button.

dismiss.description: Trailing 16px CloseIcon rendered as a bare icon button. Appears only when `onDismiss` is wired. Inherits Icon Button's hit-area floor (24-rung) without painting Icon Button chrome — the row reads as a flat brand strip, not a control cluster.

title.description: Ad headline. heading.md / onSurface. Single line; truncates.

body.description: Ad body excerpt. body.sm / onSurfaceVariant. Two-line clamp.

media.description: Hero block under the body. Square-cornered radius.md, fills the card's inline width. When `media.background` is set the block paints that fill behind the image, so a transparent product-shot reads against a brand-tinted slab.

cta.description: Full-width Standard Button pinned at the foot of the card. Carries the only commit on the placement; `appearance` defaults to `primary`. Drops out of the layout entirely when omitted — used on My Page placements where the surrounding surface already routes the tap.

states.note: AdFeed container is not itself an interactive primitive — commit lives in the trailing close affordance and the foot CTA. Each follows its own spec's state contract.

focusIndicator.description: Container is not a focus target; each nested control (dismiss, CTA) paints its own ring per its own spec.

behavior.minimalShape: Brand row + media + CTA is the minimum legible placement. `title` and `body` are both optional — when both are absent, the brand row sits directly above the media, with no reserved whitespace.
behavior.truncationNotWrap: Title truncates to one line; body clamps to two. The card does not grow to fit a long headline.
behavior.dismissOptIn: The trailing close button only renders when `onDismiss` is wired; ad placements without a user-dismiss path render the brand row at one column-of-text wide.
behavior.ctaCommitsAlone: When present, the CTA is a single full-width Standard Button at the foot — never two CTAs, never an engagement footer. Ads are not authored content.
behavior.ctaOmittedOnMyPage: On the My Page surface the entire card is the affordance (the parent route handles the tap), so the foot CTA is omitted to avoid stacking a redundant commit on top of the surface's own navigation. Other in-feed placements keep the CTA.
